items.label: April Brewer Hot
items.note: April profile recalibrated toward a tighter 240 ml service recipe with the simpler cadence often preferred behind the bar.

steps.label: Bloom
steps.note: Charge evenly and bloom for 30 seconds.

steps.label: Second Pour
steps.note: Pour to just past halfway with calm controlled circles.

steps.label: Final Pour
steps.note: Finish to target without aggressive agitation.

steps.label: Finish
steps.note: Let the bed finish draining and serve.

items.label: April Brewer Japanese Iced
items.note: Japanese iced April profile: measured ice in server, low-agitation pulses, and a quick server stir after drawdown.

steps.label: Pulse 1
steps.note: Rinse/preheat filter, keep measured ice in server, then charge evenly with a concentrated first pulse.

steps.label: Pulse 2
steps.note: Keep agitation minimal and the flat bed level.

steps.label: Pulse 3
steps.note: Finish to the hot-water target over measured ice, then stir the server 5-8 seconds.

items.label: April Hybrid Brewer Hot
items.note: Hybrid brewer stays close to April fundamentals while tightening the flow a little for travel-friendly stability.

steps.label: Pulse 1
steps.note: Start with a calm even charge.

steps.label: Pulse 2
steps.note: Repeat the pulse with the same height.

steps.label: Pulse 3
steps.note: Finish evenly and keep agitation low.

items.label: April Hybrid Brewer Iced
items.note: Iced adaptation of April Hybrid Brewer Hot with concentrated hot-phase extraction before dilution over ice.

steps.label: Pulse 1
steps.note: Start with a calm even charge.

steps.label: Pulse 2
steps.note: Repeat the pulse with the same height.

steps.label: Pulse 3
steps.note: Finish evenly and keep agitation low. Keep concentrate stable before combining with measured ice.

items.label: Blue Bottle Dripper Hot
items.note: Cafe-oriented flat-bottom profile recalibrated into a shorter, more repeatable service flow.

steps.label: Bloom
steps.note: Even saturation and 30-second bloom.

steps.label: Second Pour
steps.note: Pour to most of the target with tidy circles.

steps.label: Final Pour
steps.note: Finish to target and keep the bed level.

steps.label: Finish
steps.note: Let drawdown complete and serve.

items.note: Iced adaptation of Blue Bottle Dripper Hot tuned for concentrated hot-phase extraction before chilling.

steps.label: Final Pour
steps.note: Finish to target and keep the bed level.

steps.label: Finish
steps.note: Let drawdown complete and serve. Keep output concentrated before adding ice.

items.label: Cafec Deep 27 Hot
items.note: Deeper cone geometry usually wants a slightly looser grind and calmer pour pacing.

steps.label: Bloom
steps.note: Fully saturate the deeper bed.

steps.label: Main Pour
steps.note: Build the slurry without over-flooding.

steps.label: Mid Pour
steps.note: Keep pour height moderate.

steps.label: Finish
steps.note: Finish gently and let the cone drain completely.

steps.label: Finish

items.label: Cafec Flower Hot
items.note: Cone profile tuned around CAFEC-style flow stability and paper pairing.

steps.label: Bloom
steps.note: Saturate fully.

steps.label: Main Pour
steps.note: Use controlled spiral pulses.

steps.label: Finish
steps.note: Finish cleanly and let the rib structure do the rest.

items.label: Cafec Flower Japanese Iced
items.note: Iced adaptation of Cafec Flower Hot tuned for concentrated hot-phase extraction before chilling.

steps.label: Bloom

steps.label: Main Pour

steps.label: Finish

items.label: Chemex Hot Thick-Filter SOP
items.note: Chemex hot profile is intentionally slower than V60: strong paper rinse, warm glass, open vent, medium-coarse grind, and stable center-to-mid flow through the thick filter.

steps.label: Bloom
steps.note: Rinse the bonded paper hard, preheat the glass, keep the three-layer side toward the spout, leave the vent open, then bloom fully.

steps.label: Main Pour
steps.note: Build with a steady center-to-mid stream; do not chase the paper wall or collapse the vent.

steps.label: Top Up
steps.note: Top up with stable flow and keep slurry height calm so the thick filter drains open.

steps.label: Drawdown
steps.note: Let the thick Chemex paper finish naturally; if it stalls, go coarser next brew rather than swirling late.

items.label: Chemex Japanese Iced Thick-Filter SOP
items.note: Chemex iced is hot concentrate over measured ice only: no extra bypass water, no wall chasing, and enough drawdown time for bonded paper resistance.

steps.label: Bloom Concentrate
steps.note: Rinse the thick paper, preheat the glass, set measured ice in the server, then bloom as hot concentrate.

steps.label: Main Concentrate
steps.note: Pour a stable hot concentrate through the center; avoid paper-wall bypass and keep the vent open.

steps.label: Finish Concentrate
steps.note: Finish only to the hot-water target over measured ice; do not add bypass water after drawdown.

steps.label: Drawdown
steps.note: Let the thick filter drain over measured ice, then stir the server 5-8 seconds before serving.

items.label: Clever Dripper Hot
items.note: Clever hot is steep-and-release first: full-water charge, quiet immersion, open valve, drawdown, serve. It is not a V60 pulse recipe.

steps.label: Charge Closed
steps.note: Valve closed. Add all brew water, saturate evenly, and start immersion.

steps.label: Steep
steps.note: Hold immersion contact without more pouring or repeated stirring.

steps.label: Release
steps.note: Open the valve over the server and let the bed drain cleanly.

steps.label: Serve
steps.note: Serve after drawdown; if muddy, go slightly coarser next brew.

items.label: Clever Dripper Japanese Iced
items.note: Clever iced is hot concentrate from a closed steep released over measured ice; no bypass water is added after release.

steps.label: Charge Closed
steps.note: Valve closed. Add the full hot-water target over coffee and measured ice waits in the server.

steps.label: Steep
steps.note: Hold a short immersion contact; do not add bypass water.

steps.label: Release Over Ice
steps.note: Open the valve over measured ice and let concentrate drain cleanly.

steps.label: Stir Server
steps.note: Stir the server 5-8 seconds before serving.

steps.label: Pulse 3
steps.note: Finish to the hot-water target and chill immediately.

items.label: Cone Family Japanese Iced
items.note: Family fallback for cone brewers in Japanese iced mode: bloom, two compact build pours, final hot-water target, then stir the chilled server after drawdown.

steps.label: Bloom
steps.note: Wet all grounds with a compact bloom and give the bed about 30 seconds.

steps.label: Center Pour
steps.note: Build to about half of the hot-water target with a tight center-to-mid stream.

steps.label: Second Pulse
steps.note: Keep slurry depth modest and avoid washing the paper wall.

steps.label: Final Pour
steps.note: Finish to the hot-water target and let the cone drain over the ice before stirring the server.

steps.label: Final Pour
steps.note: Finish to target with a calm center-weighted pour.

steps.label: Finish
steps.note: Let drawdown settle and serve.

items.label: Hario Switch Hot
items.note: Hario Switch hot is hybrid immersion-first: closed bloom/fill, quiet steep, open switch, drawdown, serve.

steps.label: Closed Bloom
steps.note: Switch closed. Rinse and preheat first, then wet all grounds evenly.

steps.label: Closed Fill
steps.note: Still closed. Fill calmly to target water and avoid heavy agitation.

steps.label: Steep Closed
steps.note: Hold immersion contact quietly before opening.

steps.label: Open Switch
steps.note: Open the switch cleanly and let the bed drain without stirring.

steps.label: Serve
steps.note: Serve after drawdown; if muddy, go slightly coarser next brew.

items.label: Hario Switch Japanese Iced
items.note: Hario Switch iced uses closed hot immersion released over measured ice; no bypass water after release.

steps.label: Charge Closed
steps.note: Switch closed. Add the full hot-water target over coffee while measured ice waits in server.

steps.label: Steep Closed
steps.note: Hold a short immersion contact; do not add extra water.

steps.label: Open Over Ice
steps.note: Open the switch over measured ice and let concentrate drain cleanly.

steps.label: Stir Server
steps.note: Stir the server 5-8 seconds before serving.

items.label: Hario Switch 02 Hot Hybrid
items.note: Switch 02 hot uses a safe hybrid: closed bloom, release, open fill, short drawdown, serve.

steps.label: Closed Bloom
steps.note: Valve closed. Wet the bed evenly while staying inside the 02 chamber limit.

steps.label: Open Switch
steps.note: Open the switch before the larger fill so the 02 chamber is not overloaded.

steps.label: Open Fill
steps.note: Valve open. Fill to the final target as percolation, not a closed hold.

steps.label: Drawdown
steps.note: Let drawdown finish cleanly without topping up.

steps.label: Serve
steps.note: Serve after drawdown; go slightly coarser if the cup turns muddy.

steps.note: Open the switch over measured ice and let concentrate drain cleanly.

steps.label: Stir Server

items.label: Hario V60 Hot
items.note: Service-friendly cone baseline tuned toward a shorter, easier SOP with clean drawdown timing.

steps.label: Bloom
steps.note: Wet all grounds evenly and let bloom open for 30 seconds.

steps.label: Second Pour
steps.note: Pour to roughly 60% of total water with calm centered circles.

steps.label: Final Pour

steps.label: Finish
steps.note: Let drawdown finish cleanly and serve.

items.label: Hario V60 Japanese Iced
items.note: Tighter hot-brew mass over ice, keeping the V60 drawdown controlled; stir the chilled server after drawdown so service is not treated as another brew step.

steps.label: Bloom

steps.label: Center Pour
steps.note: Build to roughly half of the hot-water target with a calm center-to-mid pour.

steps.label: Second Pulse
steps.note: Keep slurry depth moderate and avoid washing the paper wall.

steps.label: Final Pour
steps.note: Finish to the hot-water target, let drawdown finish over the ice, then stir the server 5-8 seconds.

items.label: Kalita Wave 155 Hot
items.note: Kalita Wave 155 profile for 12-17 g doses: small flat bed, center pour, level slurry, and no wide circular agitation.

steps.label: Bloom
steps.note: Saturate the small wave bed edge to edge and let it settle level.

steps.label: Center Pour
steps.note: Pour mostly center with a small ring; keep slurry low and the bed flat.

steps.label: Final Pour
steps.note: Top up evenly without a wide spiral or last-second swirl.

steps.label: Drawdown
steps.note: Let the 155 finish flat and tidy before serving.

items.label: Kalita Wave 185 Hot
items.note: Kalita Wave 185 profile for 18-24 g doses: broader bed, slightly coarser grind, centered flat-bed pulses, and a longer finish than 155.

steps.label: Bloom
steps.note: Saturate the larger wave bed evenly and let the base settle flat.

steps.label: Main Pour
steps.note: Use center-to-small-ring flow; keep the slurry height stable instead of spiraling hard.

steps.label: Top Up
steps.note: Top up evenly across the flat bed and avoid flooding one side.

steps.label: Drawdown
steps.note: Let the wider 185 bed draw down cleanly before serving.

items.label: Kalita Wave Japanese Iced
items.note: Kalita Wave auto iced baseline; the planner now resolves 155 or 185 behavior from dose when the shared 155/185 catalog item is selected.

steps.label: Pulse 1

steps.label: Finish
steps.note: Finish to the hot-water target over measured ice, then stir server 5-8 seconds.

items.label: Kalita Wave 155 Japanese Iced
items.note: Kalita Wave 155 iced profile keeps measured ice in server and uses compact center pours for small-dose sweetness.

steps.label: Finish Concentrate
steps.note: Finish to the hot-water target over measured ice, then let drawdown complete.

steps.label: Finish Concentrate

steps.label: Finish

steps.label: Finish

steps.label: Finish

items.label: Origami Wave Filter Hot

steps.label: Final Pour
steps.note: Top up evenly and keep the bed flat without a late swirl.

steps.label: Drawdown
steps.note: Let the wave-filter drawdown finish level and clean.

steps.label: Finish Concentrate

items.label: Espresso Machine Standard Shot
items.note: Pressure-shot profile targets yield and time rather than pour pulses.

steps.label: Extract
steps.note: Start the shot and track yield, flow, and time together.

steps.label: Stop
steps.note: Stop at target yield/time and taste before changing the next shot.

items.label: French Press Immersion
items.note: French Press is full immersion: charge water, steep, settle, press gently, and decant cleanly.

steps.label: Charge
steps.note: Add all water evenly and saturate every dry pocket.

steps.label: Steep
steps.note: Steep quietly without repeated stirring.

steps.label: Settle
steps.note: Break crust gently if needed, then let fines settle.

steps.label: Decant
steps.note: Decant off the grounds so extraction stops cleanly.

items.label: French Press Clean Decant
items.note: James Hoffmann method: long steep, skim foam, surface-only mesh to avoid fines migration.

steps.label: Charge
steps.note: Pour boiling water swiftly to saturate all coffee grounds.

steps.label: Steep
steps.note: Allow the crust to form undisturbed for 4 minutes.

steps.label: Break & Skim
steps.note: Stir the crust gently, skim the surface foam and floating oils.

steps.label: Settle
steps.note: Let the coffee sit quietly for 4 minutes so all fines completely settle to the bottom.

steps.label: Decant
steps.note: Fit plunger and lower it just to touch the liquid surface; pour out extremely gently without plunging to the bottom.

items.label: French Press Double Filter
items.note: Double mesh or paper filter insert to trap all fines, allowing a finer grind profile.

steps.label: Charge
steps.note: Pour water in circular motions to saturate the medium-ground bed.

steps.label: Steep
steps.note: Steep cleanly while the double filter elements are pre-wet and prepared.

steps.label: Settle
steps.note: Give a light swirl to settle the grounds before placing the plunger.

steps.label: Plunge & Serve
steps.note: Press down slowly over 30 seconds through the double filter; serve cleanly.

items.label: French Press Heavy Concentrate
items.note: High-dose 1:8 ratio concentrate, heavy agitation, short steep for maximum mouthfeel and bypass dilutability.

steps.label: Charge
steps.note: Pour water rapidly over the heavy dose to wet the fine grounds.

steps.label: Agitate
steps.note: Stir vigorously 5-6 times to maximize early extraction strength.

steps.label: Steep
steps.note: Let the thick immersion concentrate develop body and richness.

steps.label: Press & Serve
steps.note: Press firmly to the absolute bottom of the puck, serve as concentrate or bypass.

items.label: French Press Sweet Immersion
items.note: Gently agitated, moderate temperature, slightly longer steep to maximize sweet caramel solubility.

steps.label: Charge
steps.note: Pour water gently to promote a round, sweet extraction.

steps.label: Stir
steps.note: Stir gently exactly 2 times to distribute extraction evenly.

steps.label: Steep
steps.note: Steep quietly to allow sugar compounds to fully dissolve.

steps.label: Plunge & Decant
steps.note: Plunge extremely slowly over 30 seconds to avoid fines churning, serve immediately.

items.label: AeroPress Standard
items.note: AeroPress standard style: upright charge, 3 stirs, short steep, and steady 20-30 second press that stops before the dry hiss for a balanced cup.

steps.label: Charge
steps.note: Add water quickly, wet all grounds, and start the timer.

steps.label: Stir
steps.note: Stir 3 times, then stop and let contact settle.

steps.label: Steep
steps.note: Hold short immersion contact before pressing.

steps.label: Press
steps.note: Press steadily for 20-30 seconds and stop before the dry hiss with light, even force.

items.label: AeroPress Inverted
items.note: AeroPress Inverted style: steep, defined stir count, controlled press duration, and stop cue are encoded as operational steps.

steps.label: Invert Charge
steps.note: Assemble inverted safely, add water, and wet all grounds.

steps.label: Stir
steps.note: Stir 4 times, attach cap, and prepare to flip securely.

steps.label: Flip
steps.note: Flip onto the cup/server carefully without shaking the bed.

steps.label: Press
steps.note: Press 20-30 seconds and stop before the dry hiss while keeping the cup and chamber aligned.

items.label: AeroPress Bypass
items.note: AeroPress Bypass style: steep, defined stir count, controlled press duration, and stop cue are encoded as operational steps.

steps.label: Concentrate Charge
steps.note: Brew a compact concentrate; keep bypass water separate.

steps.label: Stir
steps.note: Stir 3 times and settle briefly.

steps.label: Press
steps.note: Press 20-30 seconds, stop before hiss, then dilute in the cup.

steps.label: Bypass Dilute
steps.note: Add bypass water after pressing only; do not push it through the puck.

items.label: AeroPress No-Bypass
items.note: AeroPress No-Bypass style: steep, defined stir count, controlled press duration, and stop cue are encoded as operational steps.

steps.label: Full Charge
steps.note: Use the full brew water in the chamber; no bypass dilution later.

steps.label: Stir
steps.note: Stir 3 times, then let the slurry settle.

steps.label: Steep
steps.note: Hold contact so the full water extracts evenly.

steps.label: Press
steps.note: Press 25-35 seconds and stop before the dry hiss for a complete no-bypass cup.

items.label: AeroPress Bright Clean
items.note: AeroPress Bright Clean style: steep, defined stir count, controlled press duration, and stop cue are encoded as operational steps.

steps.label: Charge
steps.note: Add water quickly and keep the bed compact for clarity.

steps.label: Stir
steps.note: Stir 2-3 times only, then stop.

steps.label: Press
steps.note: Press 20-30 seconds and stop before the hiss for a clean finish.

items.label: AeroPress Sweet Body
items.note: AeroPress Sweet Body style: steep, defined stir count, controlled press duration, and stop cue are encoded as operational steps.

steps.label: Charge
steps.note: Add water and fully saturate the compact bed.

steps.label: Stir
steps.note: Stir 5 times to build body, then let the slurry settle.

steps.label: Steep
steps.note: Hold a longer sweet-body contact window.

steps.label: Press
steps.note: Press 25-35 seconds near the hiss, then stop before the finish turns dry or gritty.

items.label: Hario Siphon Vacuum Brew
items.note: Siphon profile follows heat, draw-up, add coffee, stir, extract, remove heat, drawdown, serve.

steps.label: Charge Water
steps.note: Load water in the lower bowl and seat the filter.

steps.label: Heat
steps.note: Apply heat until water draws up into the upper chamber.

steps.label: Add Coffee
steps.note: Add coffee once water is up and stir gently to wet the bed.

steps.label: Extract
steps.note: Hold a short extraction with only one gentle stir.

steps.label: Drawdown
steps.note: Remove heat and let vacuum draw coffee down naturally.

steps.label: Serve
steps.note: Serve while aromatics are high.

items.label: Bialetti Moka Pot Standard
items.note: Moka Pot profile uses basket dose, boiler water below the safety valve, moderate heat, and a stop before sputter. It is not a filter ratio pulse recipe.

steps.label: Charge
steps.note: Fill below the safety valve and level the basket without tamping.

steps.label: Heat
steps.note: Use low to medium heat and keep the flow calm.

steps.label: Stop
steps.note: Remove from heat as the chamber fills or sputter begins.

items.label: Toddy Cold Brew Immersion
items.note: Cold brew profile uses cool water, coarse grind, full saturation, long cold immersion, then filtering. It is intentionally not a hot workflow.

steps.label: Charge
steps.note: Add cool water in stages and saturate all grounds.

steps.label: Steep
steps.note: Cover and steep without heat or repeated agitation.

steps.label: Filter
steps.note: Filter cleanly before diluting or serving over ice.

items.label: Batch Brewer Golden Cup Service
items.note: Batch brewer profile uses dose per liter, machine brew volume, seated filter, and spray-pattern consistency instead of manual pour pulses.

steps.label: Start Machine
steps.note: Load dose per liter, set machine brew volume, seat the filter, and start the brewer.

steps.label: Main Spray
steps.note: Let the machine spray pattern extract evenly; do not manual-pulse the bed.

steps.label: Drawdown
steps.note: Watch the stream-to-drip transition for a clean finish.

steps.label: Mix and Serve
steps.note: Mix the finished batch gently before service.
